authoritative_sources.title: Auto Warranties and Auto Service Contracts
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC guidance is authoritative for distinguishing an included vehicle warranty from a separately purchased auto service contract, for maintenance-record expectations, and for unsupported warranty-denial boundaries.

authoritative_sources.title: Auto Repair Basics
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC auto-repair guidance is authoritative for completed repair-order evidence, repair warranty limitations, prior-authorization expectations for service contracts, and dispute-documentation posture.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: repair-order evidence capture and completion review

authoritative_sources.title: Resources Related to Investigations and Recalls
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NHTSA is the primary U.S. vehicle-safety regulator. This public page is authoritative for VIN recall verification, recall permanence, manufacturer-communication lookup, and complaint routing that changes release and safety boundaries.

authoritative_sources.title: Toyota Recall No. 24TA07 (Remedy Notice)
authoritative_sources.publisher: Toyota Motor Sales, U.S.A., Inc. via NHTSA
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Toyota's dealer-facing recall remedy notice is an authoritative OEM operating reference for stop-delivery, TIS verification, used-inventory disclosure, service-loaner removal, claim-filing accuracy, and proof-of-correction handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: recall hold, inventory release, and campaign completion handling

authoritative_sources.title: Bulletin Number: WB08-X-001
authoritative_sources.publisher: Isuzu Commercial Truck of America via NHTSA
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Isuzu's warranty-parts bulletin is an authoritative OEM operating reference for repair-order attachment, failed-part retention windows, requested-part return timing, waiver handling, and status-state tracking that governs chargeback risk.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: failed-part retention, parts-return workflow, and claim evidence review

authoritative_sources.title: Warranty Policy and Procedures Manual
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Aston Martin's public dealer warranty manual is an authoritative OEM reference for confirming warranty status, required auditable documentation, displaced-part labeling and storage, parts issue recording, and claim-audit boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: service reception, warranty confirmation, and audit-ready documentation control

authoritative_sources.title: Ford Motor Company Customer-Specific Requirements for IATF 16949:2016
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Ford's customer-specific requirements are authoritative public automotive quality requirements for organizations manufacturing production and service parts and explicitly cover MMOG/LE, part identification and tracking, release, concession, customer notification, and warranty-management expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: service-part traceability, release, and warranty-management boundary review

authoritative_sources.title: IATF 16949 - GM Customer-Specific Requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GM's customer-specific requirements are authoritative public automotive quality requirements for organizations manufacturing production and service parts and define service-part scope, supplier definitions, active-part status, waiver, deviation, and supplier-quality approval boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: service-part scope, supplier-quality, and deviation boundary review

authoritative_sources.title: (MMOG/LE) Global Materials Management Operations Guideline
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AIAG is the recognized automotive industry standards body. Its MMOG/LE description is an authoritative public reference for supply-chain process capability across production and post-production service phases, including inventory, delivery, and sub-supplier controls.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: inventory, delivery, and service-parts logistics coordination

authoritative_sources.title: AIAG Traceability Guideline
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AIAG's traceability guideline is a recognized public automotive traceability reference for product visibility across the supply chain, recall-scope reduction, and traceability self-assessment and fire-drill validation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: parts genealogy, recall containment, and cross-system traceability validation

workflow_stages.name: service intake and authority grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm dealer, OEM, jurisdiction, VIN, mileage, campaign, and service-contract or warranty context before the lane allows any lifecycle-state change.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: coverage, campaign, and prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Verify repair-order quality, technician and customer complaint data, warranty or service-contract prerequisites, and whether open campaigns or holds change the service path.
source_classes: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards

workflow_stages.name: parts sourcing, traceability, and execution coordination
workflow_stages.description: Track service-part issuance, causal-part assignment, lot or date-code linkage, inventory status, parts return posture, and technician execution evidence while work is in process.
source_classes: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: exception, hold, and supplier or quality escalation
workflow_stages.description: Handle repeat repairs, no-fault-found outcomes, wrong-part installs, open recalls, quality holds, failed-part return requests, and supplier or substitution pressure without absorbing formal approval authority.
source_classes: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: completion evidence and release-boundary handling
workflow_stages.description: Close service work only when repair-order completion, parts, claim, and any proof-of-correction artifacts are present, and escalate unresolved holds, campaigns, or supplier-quality decisions instead of improvising release.
source_classes: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards

artifacts: repair orders, appointments, VIN records, mileage, concern-cause-correction notes, and labor op codes
warranty terms, service contracts, prior-approval notes, campaign bulletins, and recall or proof-of-correction records
parts picks, inventory reservations, parts ETA updates, causal-part numbers, lot or date-code records, and failed-part return tags
quality holds, nonconformance reports, release records, change-control documents, and supplier alerts
technician stories, sublet records, loaner records, claim submissions, chargeback or audit notes, and customer-ready evidence
traceability logs, inventory movements, and logistics handoff records

allowed: classify service-lane work as in-scope, out-of-scope, blocked, awaiting authorization, waiting on parts, in service, in rework, on quality hold, or complete based on documented execution state and evidence
request or validate the minimum tenant facts, repair-order records, VIN context, campaign status, and parts traceability evidence needed to continue safely
prepare evidence-backed service status updates, completion records, and escalation packages without overstating authority
escalate: safety recall, stop-sale, campaign release, or proof-of-correction authority
supplier approval, alternate-part approval, or formal quality-hold disposition
legal interpretation, goodwill approval, or financial override decisions
refuse: marking service work complete without technician, parts, or customer-ready evidence
releasing or delivering a vehicle when campaign, hold, or release controls remain unresolved
bypassing VIN, repair-order, causal-part, traceability, or parts-return controls to protect lane throughput

domain_failure_modes: a vehicle is released or the claim is submitted even though the VIN still carries an open campaign or the proof-of-correction requirement is incomplete
the wrong service part or lot is tied to the repair because DMS, WMS, and return-tag records were not reconciled
failed-part retention, requested-part return, or audit-ready repair-order paperwork is skipped, causing chargeback exposure and traceability loss
a non-approved alternate supplier or non-OEM part is installed because back-order pressure is treated as a lane-owned approval decision
the lane drifts into legal, engineering, supplier-quality, or final quality-release authority instead of remaining an execution and evidence specialist
public source requirements and tenant policy are flattened into a confident answer instead of escalated for human resolution

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Supplier or substitution decision outside lane authority

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: OEM portal and claim-status naming differences by brand
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: As of April 10, 2026, public OEM references show similar service-lane concepts but different portal names, claim statuses, prior-approval rules, and failed-part return thresholds. Tenant runtime retrieval must supply the exact brand-specific workflow labels before final claim handling.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State-specific used-vehicle disclosure and warranty overlays
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: FTC and NHTSA sources provide the federal baseline, but used-vehicle disclosure, state warranty rights, and lemon-law escalation rules vary by jurisdiction. The lane therefore escalates legal or jurisdiction-specific interpretation instead of guessing.

notes: Authoritative vehicle, repair, campaign, parts, inventory, and supplier records are the DMS, OEM portal, parts, inventory, quality, and logistics systems designated by the tenant, constrained by FTC, NHTSA, OEM, IATF, and AIAG baselines.

The lane stops before formal quality release, supplier approval, engineering approval, legal interpretation, goodwill approval, or final safety-release authority even when the service drive is under pressure.
